sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.63 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: Let$_e(G)$denote the set of orders of elements of a group$G$. For${ N}$let$h( )$denote the number of non-isomorphic finite groups$G$with$_e(G)=$. Is there a number$k$such that, for every$$, either$h( ) k$, or$h( )=$? { W.\,J.\,Shi} No, there is no such number:$h( _e(L_3 (7^{3^r})))=r+1$for any$r 0$(A.\,V.\,Zavarnitsine, J.~Group Theory, { 7}, no.\,1 (2004), 81--97).
